slides.title: Numbers up to 10,000 Slide 1
slides.text: A 4-digit number is made of Thousands, Hundreds, Tens, and Ones. The value of a digit is determined by its place value. For example, in the number three thousand four hundred fifty, the digit three holds the value of three thousand, while four holds the value of four hundred. In the town of Banga, farmers recorded that they harvested nine thousand eight hundred bags of high-quality rice this month. How does the position of a digit change its total value when writing numbers up to ten thousand? Study slide 1. To understand this concept deeply, we must look at how it applies to different situations in our daily lives. By studying this topic, students can develop critical thinking skills and learn to solve complex problems. It is important to practice regularly and discuss these ideas with your peers and teachers to build a strong foundation.

examples.title: Example 1
examples.content: 4,321 = 4 thousands, 3 hundreds, 2 tens, 1 ones. Written in words as: four thousand three hundred twenty-one.

slides.title: Numbers up to 10,000 Slide 11
slides.text: We can represent numbers pictorially using base-ten blocks. A large block represents one thousand, a flat sheet represents one hundred, a rod represents ten, and a small single block represents one. Combining these blocks helps us visualize the actual quantity of large numbers. A local cooperative in Tupi represents their fruit shipments of four thousand two hundred boxes of papayas using block drawings. How do drawings of sheets and rods help us see place values like hundreds and tens clearly? Card 11. To understand this concept deeply, we must look at how it applies to different situations in our daily lives. By studying this topic, students can develop critical thinking skills and learn to solve complex problems. It is important to practice regularly and discuss these ideas with your peers and teachers to build a strong foundation.

examples.title: Example 20

slides.title: Numbers up to 10,000 Slide 21
slides.text: Writing numbers in numerals and in words is a vital skill. When writing, read the thousands place first, followed by the hundreds, and then read the tens and ones together. Be careful with spelling and ensure that you use zeros as placeholders when a place value is empty. At the General Santos City fish port, a clerk writes a receipt for eight thousand six hundred fifty boxes of tuna. Why is it important to use zero as a placeholder when writing numbers that have no hundreds or tens? Summary slide 21. To understand this concept deeply, we must look at how it applies to different situations in our daily lives. By studying this topic, students can develop critical thinking skills and learn to solve complex problems. It is important to practice regularly and discuss these.

challenge.q: I am a 4-digit number. My thousands digit is 3. My hundreds digit is 0. My tens digit is 5. My ones digit is 2. What number am I?
I am a 4-digit number. My thousands digit is 4. My hundreds digit is 0. My tens digit is 5. My ones digit is 2. What number am I?
I am a 4-digit number. My thousands digit is 5. My hundreds digit is 0. My tens digit is 5. My ones digit is 2. What number am I?
I am a 4-digit number. My thousands digit is 6. My hundreds digit is 0. My tens digit is 5. My ones digit is 2. What number am I?
I am a 4-digit number. My thousands digit is 7. My hundreds digit is 0. My tens digit is 5. My ones digit is 2. What number am I?

pages:
Exercise 1: Base-Ten Block Values

Write the number represented by the base-ten block models below.

1. 3 thousands blocks, 5 hundreds sheets, 2 tens rods, and 8 ones blocks =
3528
2. 7 thousands blocks, 0 hundreds sheets, 4 tens rods, and 0 ones blocks =
7040

Exercise 2: Writing Numerals

Write the correct standard numeral for the given number in words.

1. "Five thousand four hundred ninety-two" in numerals:
5492

Exercise 3: Place Value up to 10,000

Identify the place value of the underlined digit.

1. In the number 8,345, what is the place value of the underlined digit 3?
hundreds

performance.title: Tuna Harvest Record Block Drawing
performance.desc: Represent the number 4,325 (which represents boxes of tuna harvested at the General Santos City port) using drawings of base-ten blocks. Draw 4 large cubes for thousands, 3 flat squares for hundreds, 2 vertical rectangles for tens, and 5 small dots for ones. Color them neatly, write the number in standard form and in words, take a photo, and upload it.
labels: Number written in standard form and in words
